headline: Private Tuition Industry Worth RM6.7 Billion Raises Equity Concerns
context: The shadow education sector serving 2.4 million students highlights gaps in the formal education system.

cards.big: RM6.7 billion private tuition industry serves 2.4 million Malaysian students in 2025
cards.sub: Average family spends RM350 monthly on supplementary classes

cards.big: 82% of urban SPM students attend private tuition versus 38% in rural areas
cards.sub: Tuition access gap directly correlates with urban-rural achievement divide

cards.big: MOE allows public school teachers to run private tuition centers after hours
cards.sub: Potential conflict of interest when teachers teach same students for extra fees

cards.big: South Korea banned private academies operating past 10pm to reduce education inequality
cards.sub: Regulatory interventions attempt to level the playing field for lower-income students

cards.big: When tuition becomes necessary for passing exams it exposes formal education failure
Should public school teachers be banned from running paid tuition for their own students
